Stage 1 (Trust): The current state appears more willing to receive support, stability, or guidance.
Stage 1 (Mistrust): The current state appears guarded around trust, safety, or dependency.
Stage 2 (Autonomy): The state appears to be testing independence, control, or self-direction.
Stage 2 (Shame): The state may hesitate before action due to fear of exposure, failure, or loss of control.
Stage 3 (Initiative): The current movement suggests growing willingness to act, initiate, or enter uncertainty.
Stage 3 (Guilt): The state may suppress initiative due to overcorrection, fear of disruption, or internal hesitation.
Stage 4 (Industry): The state appears focused on competence, productivity, practice, or visible capability.
Stage 4 (Inferiority): The current state may over-compare itself against perceived standards or expectations.
Stage 5 (Identity): The state appears engaged in questions of identity, direction, role, or self-definition.
Stage 5 (Role Confusion): The visible movement suggests uncertainty between competing roles, expectations, or self-concepts.
Stage 6 (Intimacy): The state appears more open to trust, relational honesty, or mutual visibility.
Stage 6 (Isolation): The state may protect itself through distance, self-containment, or emotional withdrawal.
Stage 7 (Generativity): The current movement appears focused on contribution, guidance, building, or long-term impact.
Stage 7 (Stagnation): The state may feel emotionally paused, disconnected from contribution, or trapped in repetition.
Stage 8 (Integrity): The state appears more capable of integrating past experience into coherent meaning.
Stage 8 (Despair): The current movement may be dominated by regret, unresolved reflection, or perceived loss of meaning.
